This is a safe and supportive environment for all identities.
Check the wiki for tips and strategy guides! https://foldit.fandom.com
Stuck on a level? The wiki has walkthroughs! https://foldit.fandom.com
Streaming Foldit is a great way to spread the word about contributing to science!
Making and sharing videos of Foldit is a great way to share ideas with other players!
Check out YouTube for guides, tips, and walkthroughs!
Got a stream or video of Foldit to share? Let us know in the Foldit Discord!
Foldit players are some of the friendliest people on the internet.
Foldit has a Discord! https://tiny.cc/FolditDiscord
Shake first and then wiggle? Or wiggle first and then shake? Why not try both?
Remember: blue segments outside, orange segments inside.
Never ten points without one. If you gain a lot of points at once, there's usually another way to gain more points.
All Foldit players start out as Soloists. Join a group to become an Evolver and collaborate with other players!
Join a group to become an Evolver! Evolvers work to improve solutions shared by other players in the group.
Players have two ranks on a puzzle: their soloist score (green) and their evolver score (purple).
One of the best ways to hone your folding skills is to join a group and play with others!
Rubber bands can push too!
You can hide Voids and Clashes in the View Options.
Use the 'Show Bondable Atoms' in the View Options to find opportunities for more hydrogen bonds!
You can change the background color in the View Options.
Computers can't see which parts of the protein look good. Use Freezing to hold part of a solution in place.
Hydrophilic means 'water-loving'. Hydrophobic means 'water-fearing'.
Thanks for playing! Your efforts are really appreciated by the scientific community!
'De novo' is a Latin phrase meaning 'from the beginning', or 'from scratch'.
You can use keybinding shortcuts to quickly use your favorite tools.
Want to help protein science even when you're away from your computer? Check out Rosetta@Home!
Press Q to re-center the protein on your screen.
Colorblind mode is available in the General Options.
Want to help us beta-test new features? Check out the Devprev Update Group!
A protein is a chain of amino acids. Almost all proteins are made from just 20 different amino acid types!
A protein is a chain of amino acids. Each amino acid in the chain is called a segment, or residue.
Helices and sheets are more formally known as 'alpha helices' and 'beta sheets', but that's Greek to me!
Yes, Foldit is still in beta...
Remember to take frequent breaks!
This is a good time for a coffee break.
A low Clashing Importance setting early in a puzzle will make the protein flexible. Set the Clashing Importance in the Behavior menu.
Prediction puzzles are posted when we know which amino acids compose a protein, but we don't know the 3D structure of the protein!
Design puzzles allow you to change the amino acids of a protein to create a brand new protein!
Electron Density puzzles give you a guide to how the protein should fold, by turning X-ray crystallography data into a visible cloud!
Small Molecule Design puzzles let you create new molecules that can bind to proteins!
'Fusing' is a strategy of shaking and wiggling with different levels of Clashing Importance. Set the Clashing Importance in the Behavior menu.
Watch your protein when you Rebuild - you may notice a shape with great potential that the computer ignores!
The Remix tool picks different shapes for a part of the protein, it's easy to use and works great for Ideal Loops
A Recipe is a script that works on your protein. Recipes are written in Lua, and are good for automating repetitive tasks.
You can find Recipes in the Cookbook. To add Recipes to your Cookbook, visit https://fold.it!
A Cutpoint lets you move parts of the protein independently, but your score will be invalid until you close the Cutpoint!
There's invisible water all around your protein. Hide your hydrophobics from the outside!
A loop is any segment that isn't a helix or sheet.
Each amino acid has a different sidechain, identified by its shape and atom types.
Check out all the sidechains in the Amino Acid Gallery on the Foldit Wiki!
Foldit is an academic project, and is developed by research scientists at universities around the world!
We're always looking to improve Foldit! Please share feedback at https://fold.it!
Check the Foldit blog for the latest updates from Foldit researchers, at https://fold.it!
For detailed information about a segment, mouse-over the segment and press Tab.
If you set the Clashing Importance to 0, you can move the protein through itself! Great for early maneuvering!
